title: Legal case files: court, parties and proceeding
audience: Court & tribunal archives, legal records, government case files
intro: A case file is created by a court but is about its parties. This scenario branches single-file vs a series, records the court as creator, and — the part people skip — models the parties as Agents who are the subject of the file, so the file is findable by person, not just by case number.
question: Is a case file a Record? Who is the creator, and how do I record the parties involved?

steps.prompt: A single case file, or a series of case files?

choices.label: A single case file
choices.why: Good — one case file is one Record (its pleadings and exhibits can be Record Parts).

choices.label: A series of case files
choices.why: Good — a court's case files form a Record Set, each case a member Record.

choices.label: An Activity
choices.why: Not the file. The proceeding is an Activity you link the file to; the file itself is a Record.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Fits. The case file is a Record; its individual documents (pleadings, judgment) can be Record Parts; the court is its creator and the parties are its subjects.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Too much for one case — a Set is for the run of case files.

choices.label: Agent
choices.why: Doesn't fit. The parties and court are Agents; the file is a Record.

capture.comment: Create the case file as a Record.

steps.prompt: Model the series of case files.

choices.label: Record Set
choices.why: Fits. A run of case files is a Record Set, each case a member Record.

choices.label: Record
choices.why: Undersells it — a series is many cases. Use a Record Set.

choices.label: Record Part
choices.why: Doesn't fit — the series is an aggregation. (A pleading inside one case file is a Record Part.)

capture.comment: Create the series as a Record Set.

steps.prompt: Who created the file — and how do you record it?

choices.label: Court (Corporate Body Agent) + has_creator
choices.why: Fits. The court or registry is a Corporate Body — an Agent. Relate the file to it with has_creator; the same court Agent created all its case files.

choices.label: Put the court name in the title
choices.why: Doesn't fit. The court should be a reconcilable Agent so its whole caseload is findable together.

capture.comment: Create the court as an Agent (corporate body).
Link the case file to the court with has_creator.

steps.prompt: The case is about its parties. How do you make the file findable by them?

choices.label: Parties as Agents, linked with has_or_had_subject
choices.why: Fits. Each party (plaintiff, defendant) is an Agent, and the file is about them — relate the file to each with has_or_had_subject. Now the file surfaces when searching that person, and links to any other records about them. Repeat per party.

choices.label: List the parties in the case title only
choices.why: Doesn't fit. Names in a title can't be reconciled or linked to a party's other cases and records. Model parties as Agents and relate them as subjects.

choices.label: Parties are the creators
choices.why: Doesn't fit. The court created the file; the parties are its subjects, not its creators. Use has_or_had_subject, not has_creator, for them.

capture.comment: Create a party as an Agent.
Link the case file to the party with has_or_had_subject (repeat per party).

outcome.verdict: Case file, court and parties — findable by person.
outcome.summary: The file is a Record (or the run a Record Set); the court is its creating Agent (has_creator); and the parties are Agents the file is about (has_or_had_subject). The case stops being reachable only by case number and becomes findable by the people it concerns — and linkable to everything else about them.

links.label: See real RiC data in the graph viewer
